mnemonics: Auction Menor = All Uniquely Determined, Constant
BAD = Bidders Annoy Each Other Daily
keyPoints: Key point 1: In an auction with an arbitrary distribution of valuations, the winner's value is the maximum value a bidder is willing to pay.
Key point 2: The Nash Equilibrium in an auction is the value that each bidder is willing to pay, given the valuations of the other bidders.
Key point 3: The Vickrey-Clarke-Groves (VCG) mechanism is a truthful auction mechanism that achieves the efficient allocation of goods, even when bidders have arbitrary valuations.
